name: side-project-to-main
description: Quit your job for your side project — but do it right. Calculate your runway, assess the real risks, run a premortem on the plan, build a transition budget, and create a daily structure for self-employment. The responsible way to take the leap.

config.instruction: Calculate runway: savings, monthly expenses (personal + business), current side project revenue, growth rate. How many months can you survive with zero income? What's the minimum revenue needed to sustain? When does the side project need to hit that number?

Assess the real risks: financial (what if revenue drops?), health insurance gap, career re-entry difficulty if it fails, relationship strain, market timing, competitive threats. Rate each and define your 'pull the ripcord' triggers — when do you go back to employment?

It's 12 months from now and the side project failed. What went wrong? Write the post-mortem from the future. Common killers: ran out of money, couldn't find customers, built the wrong thing, burned out, underestimated taxes. For each scenario, what could you do NOW to prevent it?

Build a transition budget. Two phases: (1) Before quitting — save X more months, reduce expenses, line up initial clients. (2) After quitting — bare minimum monthly budget, business expenses, tax reserves (30%), health insurance, emergency fund preservation.

Build a daily structure for self-employment. Without a boss, structure is everything. Include: deep work blocks, sales/outreach time, admin, exercise, social contact, and hard stop time. Include weekly revenue review and monthly runway check.
